text.title: Resource Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 100% allocation plans that ignore meetings, email, ceremonies, breaks.
antipattern.root-cause: Plans always slip on the first sick week or holiday; chronic delivery failure that looks like execution but is planning.
antipattern.fix: Hard-cap at 80% effective in prompt and output schema; reject above-cap allocations.

antipattern.symptom: Agent reads calendar subjects to assess meeting load.
antipattern.root-cause: Calendar subjects are sensitive PII; subject lines leak to LLM context.
antipattern.fix: Strip subject lines; pass duration + recurrence only.

antipattern.root-cause: Mid-sprint reassignments break WIP and disrupt flow.
antipattern.fix: Schedule rebalancing only at sprint boundaries; flag mid-sprint requests for human.

antipattern.symptom: Skill self-assessments inflate; people rate themselves higher than recent work suggests.
antipattern.root-cause: Self-rating bias is universal; assignments based on self-rated skill mismatch reality.
antipattern.fix: Use last-shipped-feature as ground truth; require evidence link per skill level.
